---
title: 'Architecture'
description: 'The mental model: layered architecture from consumer API down through SnapcapClient, MessagingSession, Sandbox + Shims, to Snap''s bundle JS + WASM.'
---

`@snapcap/native` is a thin orchestration layer over Snap's actual web JavaScript bundle. We do not reimplement Snap's protocols — we run them. The SDK's job is to make the bundle think it's running in a browser, and to provide the I/O the bundle expects (cookies, storage, gRPC) without rerouting through a browser shell.

## Layered diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ CONSUMER (your app)                                                     │
│   import { SnapcapClient, FileDataStore } from "@snapcap/native"        │
│   const client = new SnapcapClient({ dataStore, username, password })   │
│   if (await client.isReady()) await client.listFriends()                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SnapcapClient                            (src/client.ts)                │
│  ─ owns: cookieStore, bearer, self, lazy MessagingSession               │
│  ─ public: isReady, logout, listFriends, getConversations, postStory…   │
│  ─ private: ensureMessagingSession (lazy bundle bring-up)               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │                                              │
        │  (legacy direct gRPC path)                   │  (bundle-native path)
        ▼                                              ▼
┌──────────────────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ src/api/* + src/transport/*  │    │ MessagingSession                    │
│  ─ proto encoders/decoders   │    │   (src/auth/messaging-session.ts)   │
│  ─ AtlasGw / MessagingCore   │    │  ─ session, module, codec, events   │
│  ─ gRPC-Web framing          │    │  ─ getConversationManager() etc.    │
│  ─ tough-cookie + native     │    │  ─ inbound message pump → events    │
│    fetch                     │    │  ─ Snap's natural code path         │
└──────────────────────────────┘    └─────────────────────────────────────┘
        │                                              │
        ▼                                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Sandbox (src/shims/sandbox.ts)                                          │
│   Empty vm.Context with V8 built-ins                                    │
│     + happy-dom Window own-props projected on top                       │
│     + SDK_SHIMS overrides at every I/O boundary                         │
│     + Snap-bundle stubs (chrome, requestIdleCallback, importScripts…)   │
│                                                                         │
│  SDK_SHIMS = [                            (src/shims/index.ts)          │
│    CookieContainerShim,    // happy-dom outgoing fetch → tough-cookie   │
│    DocumentCookieShim,     // JS-level document.cookie → tough-cookie   │
│    WebSocketShim,          // ws + cookie-jar + cross-realm projection  │
│    LocalStorageShim,       // local_*  via DataStore                    │
│    SessionStorageShim,     // session_* via DataStore                   │
│    IndexedDbShim,          // indexdb_<db>__<store>__<key>              │
│  ]                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │                                              │
        ▼                                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Snap's actual code                                                      │
│   ─ accounts bundle (~5 MB JS) + kameleon WASM (814 KB)                 │
│   ─ chat bundle (~1.5 MB JS) + Fidelius WASMs (12 MB + 814 KB)          │
│   ─ webpack runtimes patched to leak __webpack_require__ (=__snapcap_p) │
│   ─ MessagingSession brings up createMessagingSession harness           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │                                              │
        ▼                                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Snap's servers                                                          │
│   accounts.snapchat.com → WebLoginService                               │
│   session.snapchat.com  → WebAttestationService                         │
│   web.snapchat.com      → AtlasGw, MessagingCore, Fidelius…             │
│   aws.duplex.snapchat.com → presence/typing WS                          │
│   cf-st.sc-cdn.net      → media uploads                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## What runs where

Three layers of foreign code execute inside the sandbox's `vm.Context`:

1. **Snap's accounts bundle** (~5 MB webpacked JS) — contains the WebLoginService gRPC client, the protobuf encoders for every Janus auth message, and the loader for `kameleon.wasm`. Loaded by `auth/kameleon.ts`.
2. **The kameleon WebAssembly module** (814 KB Emscripten) — generates the attestation token Snap's server uses to decide whether the client is a legitimate browser. Booted once per process by `auth/kameleon.ts`. See [the kameleon chapter](/docs/internals/kameleon).
3. **The chat bundle** (~1.5 MB JS + 12 MB Fidelius WASM + 814 KB sibling) — contains the AtlasGw client, MessagingCoreService, the upload helpers, and the Fidelius E2E key management. Loaded lazily by `auth/chat-bundle.ts` the first time something needs it (friend list, sending a message, the `MessagingSession` bring-up).

All three run in the sandbox. The host realm's `globalThis` is never modified — see [Why we don't use happy-dom's GlobalRegistrator](/docs/internals/sandbox#why-globalregistrator-had-to-go).

## Two paths to Snap's API

Today the SDK reaches Snap's services through two separate paths, and you'll see references to both in the source:

- **Legacy direct gRPC** (`src/api/*.ts` + `src/transport/grpc-web.ts`) — the SDK encodes a protobuf, frames it as gRPC-Web, attaches the bearer + cookies, sends through Node `fetch`. Used today by `listFriends`, `searchUsers`, `addFriend`, `getConversations`, `Conversation.sendText`, `Conversation.sendImage`, `postStory`, `fetchMessages`, presence, etc. Driver: `src/transport/grpc-web.ts`.
- **Bundle-native messaging session** (`src/auth/messaging-session.ts`) — the SDK calls `En.createMessagingSession(...)` (the same function the browser SPA uses). Returns the same managers (`getConversationManager`, `getFeedManager`, `getSnapManager`, `getFriendsManager`) the SPA gets. Inbound message decryption flows through here naturally because it's Snap's own code. See [Messaging session](/docs/internals/messaging-session).

The bundle-native path is gradually replacing the direct-gRPC path. The migration is per-method, not all-at-once. Reading received messages already routes through the bundle session.

## Real network I/O leaves the sandbox

`src/transport/native-fetch.ts` snapshots Node's `fetch` at module load. Outgoing requests go straight to Node, with cookies attached by `transport/cookies.ts` and bearer by `transport/grpc-web.ts`. The bundle's own `fetch` is happy-dom's, scoped to the sandbox, and never used for real traffic — it's only there because the bundle won't run without a `fetch` global.

The exception is the bundle-native gRPC factory: `messaging-session.ts` registers its own `nativeFetch`-backed factory via `GrpcManager.registerWebFactory`, so the bundle's bundle-side gRPC traffic flows through Node fetch too. See [I/O overrides](/docs/internals/io-overrides).

## The auth flow

Three phases on cold start:

```
1. attestation
   ───────────
   kameleon.wasm reads navigator + screen + performance (in vm realm)
   → AttestationSession.instance().finalize(username)
   → 1032-char base64 token

2. WebLogin (2-step)
   ──────────────────
   POST WebLogin { username + attestation }    ← native-fetch + jar
   ← challengeData.passwordChallenge + sessionPayload

   POST WebLogin { sessionPayload + password }
   ← Set-Cookie: __Host-sc-a-auth-session     (long-lived → cookie_jar)

3. SSO bearer mint
   ────────────────
   GET /accounts/sso?client_id=…
   ← 303 Location: https://www.snapchat.com/web#ticket=<bearer>
   GET https://www.snapchat.com/web (follow redirect)
   ← Set-Cookie: sc-a-nonce, _scid, sc_at      (parent-domain → cookie_jar)
   bearer    → session_snapcap_bearer
```

After phase 3 the cookie jar holds one host-scoped refresh cookie, three parent-domain cookies that gate `web.snapchat.com` gRPC calls, and a Bearer string that pretends to be the access token. From there every API call is a framed gRPC-Web POST with `Authorization: Bearer …` and the cookie header riding along.

Then `SyncFriendData` is walked to resolve `client.self`. Persisted to the DataStore. Done — until 401, which transparently re-mints from the cookie jar. See [SSO flow](/docs/internals/sso-flow).

## Single-account-per-VM constraint

The sandbox is a process-wide singleton (`installShims()` is first-call-wins, `getSandbox()` returns the same instance forever). The bundle's `localStorage` / `sessionStorage` / `indexedDB` namespace is bound to whichever DataStore wins that race — typically the first `SnapcapClient` constructed.

For multi-tenant deployments, **run one process per account**. A medium-sized refactor would make the sandbox per-client; that work is not done.

## What's deferred

- **Sending snaps end-to-end via the bundle session.** The Fidelius encryption path is wired (the bundle does it for us), but `sendImage` and `postStory` haven't been migrated off the legacy direct-gRPC + manual-AES path yet.
- **Public inbound-message API.** The bundle session already pumps decrypted messages through `MessagingSession.events`; surfacing this as a stable `client.onMessage(...)` is in flight.
